title: Planner-Executor
description: Planner-executor separates planning work into reviewable slices from executing those slices so independent factory streams can move in parallel.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: Planner-executor is a technique that separates planning from execution so independent factory streams can move without a global phase gate.

whyItMatters.title: Why It Matters
whyItMatters.body: Planner-executor matters when multiple agent lanes should keep moving on ready work instead of waiting for one shared planning phase to finish.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.body: A planner turns a customer ask into reviewable work slices; executors implement those slices while the planner re-enters through loopback or queue health rather than blocking the whole factory.

comparedToNearbyTechniques.title: Compared To Nearby Techniques
comparedToNearbyTechniques.body: Planner-executor is not the same as writer-reviewer, workqueue-executor, or classify-execute: it splits plan-from-execute so parallel streams can proceed without a global phase gate.
